section_title: INTRODUCTION TO THE ICT SCOUT SNIPER FIELD GUIDE
content:
This module is the Scout Sniper Enlisting Module for the ICT Scout Sniper Field Guide, Vol. 1. It is
designed for individuals unfamiliar with ICT concepts, offering a broad, comprehensive approach to hands
- on, in-the-trenches application.

For years, conceptual teaching has been available, but the step-by-step employment of concepts has
evaded most individuals. This is often due to a failure to put in the work and exercises, specifically:
- Not utilizing concepts in a demo account daily.
- Not familiarizing oneself with generic price action concepts.
Each previous video was meant to focus attention on one specific component of understanding price action
and the application of ICT tools.

This eight-part series will begin with a lot of 'dry stuff.' Similar to sniper school, you must learn the theory
(trajectory, wind, etc.) before ever taking a shot. The 'boring stuff' is essential before getting to the charts
and practical applications everyone looks forward to.

This module will cover:
- The trading platform used in ICT teachings.
- Practice drills to employ in this course.
- A general overview of the type of trades this approach specializes in.
- Realistic expectations to adopt.
- An introduction to the methodology (ammo) and usual targets.
- A homework assignment: 'The Mark'.

The trading platform utilized for analysis and teachings is Forex.com Ltd. Viewers can Google 'Forex.com
Ltd' and download the MT4 (MetaTrader 4) platform for free. This allows everyone to use a universal
medium for learning and applying concepts.

All tools and concepts shared are for employment and use in a demo account setting only. ICT does not
advocate using live funds. Everything learned here is for personal development. ICT is not an introducing
broker, trade advisor, and has no affiliation with Forex.com Ltd or any other brokerage firm. Viewers are
advised to use a demo account for practice purposes only.

section_title: OPTIMAL DEMO ACCOUNT SETUP AND PHILOSOPHY
content:
When opening a demo account with Forex.com Ltd, or any other platform, choose an account balance that
you would realistically employ if you were to go to live trading. This practice allows for a more authentic
learning experience. The tools and indicators shared are plug-and-play with MT4, making it a suitable
platform despite not being mandatory for live trading.

Sniper setups are characterized by precision point entry prices, applicable for use with limit order entry.
- Limit orders are crucial for getting the best possible entry price.
- They are very useful for controlling overall risk and market exposure.
- While they don't eliminate risk, they help in determining the most precise entry price compared to market
orders and their associated slippage.

Further characteristics of sniper setups include:
- Utilization of specific time of day criteria: The Forex market sets up routinely at specific times for price
swings.
- Coupling precision entry with time of day: This combination defines a sniper setup, as you know exactly
what to look for in advance.
- Advanced knowledge of target prices: Anticipating where price may reach for positive exit points and
specific price levels for exiting demo trades.
- Employment of strict risk management protocols: Determining how much money to risk, and how to
quickly remove and control risk throughout the trade.

A crucial element is calm and focused disciplinary trading, free from emotions and impatience. This level of
trading is difficult to obtain for most, but it comes with:
- Time.
- Strict adherence to the trading process.
- Finding your niche as a trader (e.g., specific timeframe like short-term intraday scalper, swing trader,
short-term trader, or position trader).
- Tools and concepts will be provided to help discern your personal niche.

OPPORTUNITIES
It is essential to hold realistic expectations:
- You will lose money (live and/or demo) with these concepts. No one can guarantee 100% profitable
trading.
- You will not profit on every setup. Even textbook conditions can fail.
- You will miss opportunities due to sleep, attention, or physical absence from charts. Missed trades are not
a reason to be discouraged; more opportunities will always come.

There is no promise of wealth or an easy journey to riches; this requires work.
- The concepts will lead to a better understanding of trading.
- Individual traders will not have identical results. Do not compare notes on profitability.
- Trading is unique and personal. The tools are universally shared, but personal understanding and
blending with individual comfort levels will make them your own.
- Different timeframes (e.g., 30-minute vs. 4-hour charts) yield significantly different magnitudes of moves,
making direct comparisons unreasonable.

section_title: THE PERSONAL JOURNEY AND TIME COMMITMENT
content:
Trading is not a team sport; it's a unique and personal experience.
- While a learning environment can be cultivated, actual execution, drawdown, and profitable results are
entirely personal.
- You must accept this personal journey and always be a student of price.
- Focus on success measured in small, incremental steps.
- Submit the necessary time: a minimum of six months of dedicated study and practice is required. Without
this commitment, you are wasting your time.
METHODOLOGY:

The methodology shared is based on a principle learned in 1994: the 'Triple Screen' method, found in
Alexander Elder's book 'Trading for a Living'.
- This book was discovered at a traders' library.
- The premise is to determine the likely direction of the market and then only enter trades in that direction.
- While ICT has made subtle changes, Elder's fingerprint is evident in these concepts. He is considered the
first mentor through his writings.

It is highly recommended to read the entire 'Trading for a Living' book, not just the Triple Screen chapter.
- The psychological chapters are particularly insightful for overcoming impatience, OCD, attention deficit,
and 'loser's mentality' in trading.
- The book is 'chock-full of wonderful insights,' with the Triple Screen and psychological write-ups being the
crowning jewels.
- Another major influence on ICT's work comes from Larry Williams.

- INITIAL CHART STUDY
The homework assignment is 'The Mark': acquire, scope up, and takedown a target.
- While not a large list of technical things, it's a study assignment.
- You can use any currency pair, but for this example, the EUR/USD (Fiber) is used.
- You will be looking at a specific price model in detail throughout this 8-part series.
- Start studying the EUR/USD 15-minute chart from August 2013 (at the time of recording, August 14th).

To set up your MT4 chart:
- Open a EUR/USD pair chart.
- Double-tap the chart header to maximize it.
- Change to a candlestick chart.
- Remove the grid by right-clicking and selecting 'Grid' or Ctrl+G.
- Change the color scheme to 'Black on White' in the chart properties (right-click, Properties, Common tab).
- Hit the Shift key to send the chart back from the right edge, creating open space. Adjust the triangular
marker to control the amount of open space.
15
- MINUTE CHART STUDY:

For the 15-minute chart, specifically August 2013:
- Study the chart and identify what you see after a period of time.
- Look for a specific price model and repeating themes of what price is doing and how it could be
anticipated in the future.
- Note specific criteria that repeat in the way price moves from one swing point to the next.
- Share your findings on the FX Dears.com forum thread associated with this video series.

section_title: UNDERSTANDING SWING POINTS AND USING FRACTALS (INITIAL TOOL)
content:
Swing points are defined as:
- A low with higher lows on either side of it.
- A high with lower highs on either side of it.
To help identify swing points, you can use the Bill Williams' Fractals indicator in MT4:
- Go to 'Insert' -> 'Indicators' -> 'Bill Williams' -> 'Fractals'.
- Use the default red color and the suggested size setting.
- The arrowheads delineate a point where one candle is surrounded by two higher/lower candles (for swing
low/high respectively).

A fractal high marker signifies a candle where:
- The candle to its left has a lower high.
- The candle to the left of that also has a lower high.
- The two candles to its right have highs lower than the high of the marked candle.
Similarly, a fractal low marker signifies a candle where it has two higher low candles to its left and two
higher low candles to its right.
- Note: ICT does not require waiting until two candles after the highest/lowest form, as this can often lead to
missing optimal entry points.

After observing the Fractals indicator, remove it from the chart.
- The exercise is to train your eye to see these same reference points without the tool.
- You need to learn to see these forming in real-time.
- Observe that every turning point or swing in the marketplace exhibits this generic pattern: a high with two
lower highs surrounding it, or a low with two higher lows surrounding it. This characteristic defines the
natural ebb and flow of the market.

The second sample size for study is the EUR/USD hourly chart.
- Focus on the beginning of July 2013, specifically July 1st to July 9th (the first week of July).
- Use the Ctrl+Y shortcut on your keyboard to add vertical delineating bars, which mark Sundays (double
lines) and individual trading days.
- The goal is to study this entire pocket of price action. Do not change any default settings yet on your MT4
platform.

section_title: IMPORTANCE OF INITIAL STUDY AND FUTURE INSIGHTS
content:
This initial study phase, though potentially 'boring,' is crucial. If you put in the work now, your understanding
of these two sample sizes of price data will be 'amazing' and 'flooring' by the end of this video series. You
will gain a far greater understanding of price action than from any other resource.

For both sample sizes of price data, focus on these questions:
- What time of day did every swing point occur? (MT4 displays GMT time at the bottom of the chart.)
- What price levels did these turning points occur at?
- What direction (up or down) made the most pips?
- What characteristics were synonymous with almost every single one of these turning points?
Break down your study from the hourly chart to a 15-minute chart basis, and then to a 5-minute basis for
more insight. Take detailed notes in a dedicated notepad these notes will be invaluable.

To aid in your study and documentation:
- Use the 'File' -> 'Save as Picture' -> 'Active workspace' feature in MT4 to generate an image file of your
chart.
- Print this image out.
- Physically draw lines on it with a pencil and ruler.
- Mark support/resistance levels.
- Add notations for time and price.
- Don't be afraid to be 'messy' initially; it's advisable to keep charts clean only after understanding what
you're looking for.
- Choose any single currency pair (e.g., EUR/USD, GBP/USD, USD/JPY, AUD/USD, GBP/JPY, or the Dollar
Index) to get 'real close' to price action.
LONG
- TERM HOMEWORK GOAL:

The goal is to collect at least 30 trading days' worth of sample size data (this may take slightly more than
one calendar month).
- Do this for both a 5-minute chart and a 15-minute chart for every single day.
- Mark off every time the market turns intraday, makes the high/low of the day.
- Reference the time it occurs and the time it ends the opposite end of the range (e.g., time of day for high,
time of day for low).
- Completing this exercise for 30 days will provide an 'immense understanding of price action' and what
routinely happens in the financial markets.
